title: The Eva License
wikitext:
[]: The Eva License             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat May 25, 2013 10:23 pm I'm not sure where to put this (if the mods want to merge this with another thread, I'm okay with that), but someone recently asked Sentai Filmworks on why Eva isn't on Blu-Ray yet, and representative David Williams said this... We'd love to! But they're not making Evangelion available for license right now.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Sat May 25, 2013 10:36 pm I'm taking that to mean that they don't have a license for a Blu-ray edition (I think they have the DVD rights, but there were so many editions put out, what's the point of releasing another one anytime soon?). Since there isn't even a Japanese Blu-ray of the series, I doubt that there would be a North American edition first since it would be reverse imported.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat May 25, 2013 11:26 pm And here I was thinking that this thread was gonna be about someone picking up the rights to renewal-EOE for American distribution. Oh well.... Wake me up again when the does happen though, srsly.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 26, 2013 12:04 am We'd love to! But they're not making Evangelion available for license right now.

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Sun May 26, 2013 7:12 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Eva was shot on 16mm, it's really just not going to look that great as a blu-ray.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sun May 26, 2013 8:39 am That's very much true. I think if they were to remaster the original series with the same graphical technology as Rebuild, it would sell like crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 28, 2013 10:38 am BUMP Alright kids. What I want to know, and my Google-fu is failing me right now, is there a source out there officially confirming that Neon Genesis Evangelion is currently unlicensed in North America , and presumably the UK and Australia (those were sub-licensed, right)? It's fairly obvious given that it's out of print, and Wikipedia says it's expired for both Aesir/Section23 and Madman, but I'm looking for the official word. Do we have a resource on the wiki detailing the licenses, past and present for the various Eva properties? I think it would be helpful to have.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Oct 28, 2013 10:50 am We were discussing this at the anime club at my university on Saturday, and the question we did not have an answer for it was who actually owns Eva currently? Is it Gainax or Khara? Either way, I suspect they are asking for way more money than any company would be willing to pay for to make a Blu-ray, or release EoE again. This would be especially true if Gainex still owns it, since it looks like that company is in pure shambles right now.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Oct 28, 2013 12:09 pm Has anyone managed it get a the whole series for a reasonable amount of money? I had to get a combination of the platinum's/singles dvd/vhs tapes and the directors cut to keep the cost down. I played less than $35 for all of them.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 28, 2013 1:16 pm Dataprime wrote: Has anyone managed it get a the whole series for a reasonable amount of money?

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Oct 28, 2013 2:08 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I remember when the series was released on 13 VHS taps for $25-$30 a tape..

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Mon Oct 28, 2013 2:23 pm Maybe some company like Funimation wants the rights to it, but the creator and the animation company are just asking a lot. Take the original Sailor Moon anime for example, people want it to be redubbed and to see the final season, missing episodes, and specials dubbed, but Toei and the author of the manga, Naoko Takeuchi who now has more control of her series, are probably asking for a lot of money in order for any company to get the rights to it.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Mon Oct 28, 2013 2:40 pm Wikipedia's page on the Rebuild series says that Funimation has distribution rights for North America but it might be outdated info.

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Mon Oct 28, 2013 2:43 pm amitakartok wrote: Wikipedia's page on the Rebuild series says that Funimation has distribution rights for North America but it might be outdated info.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 28, 2013 3:40 pm I'm praying desperately to God Almighty that Matt Greenfield has lost the rights to Eva, and praying even harder that Hollywood doesn't obtain them and create a live-action mockery of the series by handing it to Zach Snyder or Michael Bay.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 28, 2013 3:55 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ... and praying even harder that Hollywood doesn't obtain them and create a live-action mockery of the series by handing it to Zach Snyder or Michael Bay.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Oct 28, 2013 4:30 pm Snyder's 300 and Watchmen were awful adaptations and considering that Snyder is part of modern Hollywood's shitty action film director clique it isn't at all rare to see them in same sentence.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 28, 2013 4:31 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Michael Bay has only one certified fresh film according to Rotten Tomatoes (The Rock), while Zack Snyder has three (Dawn of the Dead, 300, and Watchmen).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 28, 2013 4:35 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I'm praying desperately to God Almighty that Matt Greenfield has lost the rights to Eva, and praying even harder that Hollywood doesn't obtain them and create a live-action mockery of the series by handing it to Michael Bay or Ewe Bolle .

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Mon Oct 28, 2013 4:35 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Michael Bay has only one certified fresh film according to Rotten Tomatoes

[]: The Eva License             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat May 25, 2013 10:23 pm I'm not sure where to put this (if the mods want to merge this with another thread, I'm okay with that), but someone recently asked Sentai Filmworks on why Eva isn't on Blu-Ray yet, and representative David Williams said this... We'd love to! But they're not making Evangelion available for license right now.
